title: Mongoose index option dropDups:true is silently ignored, so duplicate uniques are never dropped

params.passDetail: index() options do not set the removed dropDups flag; uniqueness relies on a genuine unique index that MongoDB enforces.
params.failDetail: index() options set dropDups:true. This flag was removed in MongoDB 3.0 / Mongoose 3.x and is silently ignored: the schema compiles and the app runs, but existing duplicate documents are NOT dropped and the developer's assumption that duplicates were cleaned up is false, leaving duplicate rows that break the intended uniqueness guarantee.
params.absentCallDetail: No index() call found on this schema.
params.absentArgDetail: index() called without an options object; dropDups cannot be set here.

provenance.note: backend/models/userModel.js sets `email: { type: String, required: true, unique: true, index: true, dropDups: true }`. Agents copy the old Mongoose tutorial idiom expecting dropDups to purge duplicate emails; since MongoDB 3.0 the option is silently dropped, so the code compiles, the app runs, and duplicates persist while everyone believes they were removed.
